Pic: Getty Images
Sat, Jun
13

Dagashiya Conversation Class: Learn Japanese With Retro Candy

12:00pm – 6:00pm
¥0 – ¥1,000
Hotel Options
Food
Living
Workshop
Akihabara
  • Home
  • Events
  • Events in June
Written by Patrick Balfe

Step inside a dagashiya and master basic Japanese conversation skills at this interactive class in Akihabara.

What to expect

Do you spend hours on Duolingo but crumble when stepping up to the cash register? This basic Japanese class will simulate real-world conversation in a Japanese dagashiya retro sweets store at WeLearn 80s Cafe.

Dagashi are small, inexpensive snacks and candies that generations of Japanese children grew up with, famous for their nostalgic charm, playful packaging, and unusual flavors.

Suggested Activity
Earn extra income as a clinical study participant!
Help us improve health care in Japan. Join our information session and receive 10,000 yen plus a free health checkup with no commitment. Tourist and student visas welcome!
Click here for details

In this class, you’ll play both customer and shop owner as you practice useful everyday Japanese phrases in a fun and relaxed setting. The event will run from midday until 6 p.m., and you’re welcome to pop in at any time.

What you’ll do:

  • Practice simple Japanese conversation
  • Learn useful shopping phrases
  • Try nostalgic Japanese snacks and candy
  • Experience a fun part of Japanese culture
  • Enjoy conversational role-playing in a relaxed atmosphere

Who this class is for:

  • Japanese beginners
  • Travelers and international residents
  • People interested in Japanese culture
  • Anyone who loves snacks and games

No Japanese fluency is required; beginners are welcome. Enjoy Japanese snacks, meet new people, and pick up some new language skills along the way.

Entry

Class participation is ¥500 plus one drink (¥500), paid at the door by cash, credit card, PayPay, or transit cards like Pasmo, Suica, or ICOCA.

Members enjoy free or discounted access to all events and lessons, no café charge, a 7-day free trial, and the option to cancel at any time. You can sign up via the official website.

Members or free-trial subscribers can join this event for just the one-drink fee, which starts at ¥200.

Please note that if you are not a subscription member, an additional drink fee (¥500) will apply if you stay at the café before or after the event. Members or free-trial subscribers will be asked to pay an additional drink fee of ¥200–¥500 for pre-event café use only. (More details here).

** Please note that photos taken during events at 80’s Cafe or WeLearn Community may be used for promotional purposes, including on their website and social media platforms.

Organizers may cancel events, alter schedules, or change admission requirements without notice. Always check official sites before heading to an event.
Open with Maps

Workshop
Official site
Akihabara
  • 270 m from Awajichō Station Marunouchi Line (M19)
  • 296 m from Ogawamachi Station Shinjuku Line (S7)
  • 337 m from Shinochanomizu Station

We do our best to make sure event info is correct. However, all event details are subject to change.
About Tokyo Cheapo

About

With 500,000 - 800,000 readers each month, Tokyo Cheapo is the site of choice for value hunters who want their yen to go further in Tokyo. We cover all the basics including things to do in Tokyo, how to get from Narita airport to Tokyo, which JR rail pass to choose, getting a SIM card and pocket wifi.

To find why we started this site, check out the Tokyo Cheapo Manifesto. For more about the people behind this site, check out the profiles of the cheapos. If you want to contribute, see our careers page. For advertising and sponsorship of our Tokyo Cheapo channels, get in touch here.

Affiliate Links

Some of the articles on this site contain affiliate links. If you purchase from or sign up with some companies we link to, we will be compensated. We don't endorse any of the companies we link to but we are selective in terms of the companies that we choose. You should exercise careful judgement with any online purchase. Additionally, the terms of all offers are subject to change without notice.

Sponsored Content

Sponsored posts are clearly labeled with a "Sponsored" tag. Additionally, content such as tours and tickets, hotels and apartments are commercial content.

For more on the Tokyo Cheapo story, go to our About Page.
